what_it_does: Acceptance test that exercises the ui://→code://→scope[] resolver, verifies code:// is registered as a backend scheme, real addresses resolve to symbols and files sourced from design/_system/code-symbols.json, empty scope means deny-all, malformed ui:// addresses raise loudly, and the resolver is reachable from runtime/bridge.py's /api/scope route.
observations: The top-level docstring frames the test as S3 establishing code:// in the backend and enforcing a one-source rule via the corpus registry.
It loads design/_system/code-symbols.json and design/_system/addresses.json directly from disk.
It instantiates FsStore, NodeRegistry, and Suite against a temporary store and the nodes directory.
A global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper drive all assertions and print ok lines.
It checks SCHEMES membership and scheme() parsing for code://.
It resolves ui://inbox/build-review, ui://inbox/coa, and ui://canvas/portal-window against expected symbols and scope files.
It includes a post-App.tsx-carve proof for ui://toolbar/run resolving to code://Toolbar and code://suite/run with the registry-resolved path canvas/app/src/regions/Toolbar.tsx.
It proves deny-all via an absent address returning empty scope and a surfaced note.
It exhaustively calls resolve_scope on every address in addresses.json and records any crash.
It greps bridge.py source for /api/scope and resolve_scope to prove reachability.
standards: The beyond-clean proof for ui://toolbar/run explicitly checks that the resolved path is the registry-resolved file, not the corpus shorthand Toolbar.tsx.
Empty-scope deny-all is proven with an absent address rather than assuming CSS selectors are unreferenced.
The exhaustive crash-free loop over all corpus addresses is the strongest robustness signal.
conventions: Follows an acceptance-test style by importing and exercising real runtime components rather than mocking them.
Uses a global PASS counter and a single check helper instead of a test framework's assertion methods.
Reads corpus JSON files directly instead of going through the store/registry abstraction.
Uses a broad except Exception in the exhaustive loop intentionally to prove no unhandled exception escapes.
concerns: Hard-coded expected symbols and scope files make the test brittle to corpus regeneration and carve refactors.
The broad except Exception swallows all errors and only reports the first two crashes.
Reachability of /api/scope is proven by source grep, not by calling or importing the route.
The test depends on a specific post-App.tsx-carve corpus state described only in comments.
Global PASS and print-based reporting make it unclear whether this is meant for pytest or standalone execution.
notes: An acceptance test that locks the ui://→code://→scope[] resolver behavior against the regenerated corpus registry, including carve-drift updates.
questions: What is the exact contract/fields of the dict returned by Suite.resolve_scope?
How are addresses.json and code-symbols.json regenerated and kept in sync with the source tree?
Is the intended malformed-address behavior to raise ValueError, TypeError, or a more specific address error?
Is this module intended to run under pytest or as a standalone script?
apparent_themes: Fail-safe default of empty scope as deny-all
Fail-loud grammar validation for malformed addresses
Robustness over all real corpus addresses
Acceptance testing against real on-disk corpus state
intention_signals: Establish and guard the address-scope resolver as a trustworthy backend primitive for L1 comment→build-intent and L5 self-change→element, ensuring it never hallucinates symbols or paths and never crashes on real addresses.
novelty: It proves resolved paths are registry-resolved files rather than shorthand-parsed strings by sourcing the forward ui→code map from inverted referenced_by[] entries, and it explicitly tests carve-drift updates.
contribution: A behavioral contract/acceptance test for the address-scope resolver that documents expected mappings, guards against fabricated scopes, and verifies crash-resilience and bridge integration.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the backend ui://→code://→scope[] resolver that verifies scheme registration, registry-sourced symbol and file resolution, deny-all empty scope, exhaustive crash-free behavior over corpus addresses, malformed-address loud failure, and reachability from bridge.py's /api/scope route.
intention_for_embedding: To lock the resolver's behavior against the regenerated corpus registry so L1/L5 callers can trust ui:// addresses map to real code symbols and files without crashing or fabricating scope.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that increments PASS and prints an ok line.
symbols.description: Defines a global PASS counter, asserts the supplied condition with a labeled failure message, increments PASS on success, and prints a formatted ok line. It is the only symbol in this top-level test script.
